specific_mechanism: HOPT: Hodge-Orthogonal Per-Batch Gradient Attenuator. For each minibatch B={x_i}, compute per-example gradients g_i = âˆ‡L(Î¸; x_i). Form the data-indexed vector field {g_i}_{iâˆˆB}. Apply discrete-Hodge decomposition (via graph Laplacian on a K-nearest-neighbor graph of inputs): g_i = g_i^âˆ‡ + g_i^curl + g_i^H. Update step uses (1/|B|) Î£_i (g_i^âˆ‡ + Î³_curl g_i^curl + Î³_H g_i^H) with Î³_curl=0.3, Î³_H=0.
llm_application: HOPT (Hodge-Orthogonal Per-Batch attenuator): At each step, for batch B build KNN graph G on input embeddings (k=8). Compute graph Laplacian L_G. Decompose per-example gradients via L_G-eigenspaces: gradient component (low L_G-eigenmode), curl component (high-eigenmode), harmonic (kernel of L_G). Apply per-component scaling Î³_âˆ‡=1, Î³_curl=0.3, Î³_H=0 before averaging. Optimizer otherwise standard AdamW.
novelty_claim: Hodge decomposition on per-example minibatch gradients with selective component attenuation for LLM training is unusual. Existing work uses Hodge for simplicial neural networks and signal processing, not for SGD gradient noise filtering.
motivation_justification: Hodge decomposition is a closed-form LÂ²-orthogonal split of vector fields. The candidate transfers the same operator to the batch-gradient vector field.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: KNN graph over batch inputs
M_2: Graph-Laplacian Hodge decomposition of per-example gradients
M_3: Per-component scaling Î³_âˆ‡, Î³_curl, Î³_H
M_4: Recombined average gradient as update direction
content_words: vector field gradient curl harmonic split
